characters.name: Aberama Gold
characters.description: Aberama GoldBiographical informationFull nameAberama GoldBorn1877Age52OriginEngland, United KingdomPersonal informationStatusDeceasedDied7th December 1929Cause/reasonStabbed to death by an IRA soldier, after being involved in the attempted assassination ofOswald MosleyAffiliationPeaky BlindersOccupationHitmanRelationshipsWifeUnnamed Wife †FiancéePolly Gray†(1929)SonsBonnie Gold†DaughtersEsmeralda GoldUnnamed daughterUnnamed daughterGrandfathersUnnamed grandfather †Appearance informationSeries4,5,6First seenThe NooseLast seenBlack Day(flashback)Appeared in11 episodesPortrayed byAidan GillenClassificationFormer; recurring character Aberama Goldwas a Romany Gypsy hitman-for-hire, a man with a reputation for being a dangerous savage with little regard for the rules. He was the head of a very old and well-respected Romany family. He was hired byThomas ShelbywhenLuca Changretta, set on revenge against the entireShelby family, arrives in England. Aberama tells Tommy that he does not want just money in return for his help. He also demands that Tommy to help his son,Bonnie Gold, become a boxing champion.

characters.description: Ada ThorneBiographical informationBorn1893Age47OriginSmall Heath, Birmingham, EnglandAlso known asMrs. ShelbyMrs. ThorneAda ShelbyPersonal informationStatusDeceasedDied21 November 1940Cause/reasonShot in the head by John Beckett.AffiliationShelby FamilyPeaky BlindersThe Communist Party(formerly)OccupationLibrarian(1924)Businesswoman(1924-1934)Political(1934-1940)RelationshipsHusbandFreddie Thorne†(1919-1921)BoyfriendBen Younger†(-1929)FatherArthur Shelby Sr.†MotherMrs. Shelby†BrothersArthur Shelby†Tommy Shelby†John Shelby†Finn ShelbySonsKarl ThorneDaughtersElizabeth YoungerUnclesCharlie Strong(non-biological)AuntsPolly Gray†NephewsDuke ShelbyCharles ShelbyBilly ShelbyNiecesKatie ShelbyRuby Shelby†GrandmothersBirdie Boswell†First cousinsMichael GrayAnna GrayOther relativesLaurence GraySisters-in-lawMartha Shelby(??)Esme Shelby(1919-1925)Linda Shelby(1923-1929)Grace Shelby(1924)Lizzie Shelby(1926-)Others in-lawGina Gray(1929-)Appearance informationSeries1,2,3,4,5,6First seenEpisode 1.1Last seenLock and KeyAppeared in36 episodesPortrayed bySophie RundleClassificationPresent; main character Peaky BlindersThe Communist Party(formerly) Ada Thorne(néeShelby) is the tritagonist ofPeaky Blinders. She was the only daughter ofArthur Shelby Sr.andMrs. Shelby, the younger sister ofArthurandThomas Shelby, the older sister ofJohn, andFinn Shelby, and the widow of known communist,Freddie Thorne, with whom she has a son,Karl Thorne— named after Karl Marx —, she was also the lover of the colonelBen Youngerwith whom she has a daughter,Elizabeth Younger.

characters.name: Al Capone
characters.description: Al Caponeis a Chicago mob boss and ally of thePeaky Blinders. He is an unseen character, based on the real life mobster. Capone was born in New York City in 1899 to Italian immigrant parents. He joined the Five Points Gang as a teenager and became a bouncer in organized crime premises such as brothels. In his early twenties, he moved to Chicago and became a bodyguard and trusted factotum for Johnny Torrio, head of a criminal syndicate that illegally supplied alcohol—the forerunner of the Outfit—and was politically protected through the Unione Siciliana. A conflict with the North Side Gang was instrumental in Capone's rise and fall. Torrio went into retirement after North Side gunmen almost killed him, handing control to Capone. Capone expanded the bootlegging business through increasingly violent means, but his mutually profitable relationships with mayor William Hale Thompson and the city's police meant he seemed safe from law enforcement. He developed a rivalry with New York bootleggerLuca Changretta, during the 1920's Capone was born in New York City in 1899 to Italian immigrant parents. A conflict with the North Side Gang was instrumental in Capone's rise and fall. Torrio went into retirement after North Side gunmen almost killed him, handing control to Capone. Capone expanded the bootlegging business through increasingly violent means, but his mutually profitable relationships with mayor William Hale Thompson and the city's police meant he seemed safe from law enforcement. He developed a rivalry with New York bootleggerLuca Changretta, during the 1920's

characters.name: Alfie Solomons
characters.description: "So what, they took your boy, did they? Eh? They got your boy? AND WHAT FUCKING LINE AM I SUPPOSED TO HAVE CROSSED?! How many fathers, right, how many sons, yeah, have you cut, killed, murdered, fucking butchered, innocent and guilty, to send straight to fucking Hell, ain't ya?! JUST LIKE ME! You fucking stand there, you, judging me, stand there and talk to me about crossing some fucking line? If you pull that trigger, right, you pull that trigger for a fucking honorable reason. LIKE AN HONORABLE MAN! Not like some fucking civilian, that does not understand the wicked way of our world, mate."Alfie toThomas Shelby Alfie SolomonsBiographical informationBorn1888 (52 years old)OriginCamden Town, London, EnglandAllegianceJewish of Camden TownStatusAliveRelationshipsParentsMrs. Solomons (mother)Alfred Solomons Sr (father) Charles Solomons (uncle)SpouseEdna (Only mentioned in Series 6, Lock and Key)Military serviceServiceBritish ArmyYears of service1914-1918BattlesFirst World WarAppearance informationSeries2,3,4,5,6First seenEpisode 2.2Last seenLock and KeyAppeared in13 episodesPortrayed byTom HardyAlfred "Alfie" Solomons Jr.is a Jewish gang leader who resided inCamden Town, London. It is implied that Alfie's mother was a Russian refugee,  where his mother was from and he went to school in London withDarby Sabini. Alfred Solomons Sr (father) Charles Solomons (uncle)

characters.name: Angel Changretta
characters.description: Angel ChangrettaBiographical informationBornc. 1886Ageapprox. 38Personal informationStatusDeceasedDied24th February 1924AffiliationSpinietta Crime FamilyThe Changretta Crime FamilyOccupationGangsterRestaurant ownerRelationshipsRomancesLizzie Stark(formerly)FatherVicente Changretta(deceased)MotherAudrey ChangrettaBrothersLuca Changretta(deceased)UnclesFunacci ChangrettaOther relativesUnnamed cousinAppearance informationSeries3First seenEpisode 3.2Last seenEpisode 3.2 Restaurant owner Angel Changrettais a member of theChangretta Family. He is the second son of Italian gangsterVicente Changrettaand his wifeAudrey, and the younger brother ofLuca Changretta. Angel is alsoFunacci'snephew. Angel was born as the second son of the mob boss Vicente Changretta and his wife Audrey. Angel had an older brother,Luca, who was born in America, unlike Angel himself, who was born in England. As the youngest son, when he became an adult, Angel joined to his father's illegal business and became a caporegime. At some point, his older brother killed a cashier during a robbery, resulting in his being sentenced to death. However, his father saved Luca's life by arranging for his exile to the United States. After this, Angels became the de facto heir to the Changretta family's business in England. Throughout his criminal career, Angel used five different aliases to carry out his crimes and established ties to several English criminal organizations, includingDarby Sabini. When the Peaky Blinders came to power, the Changretta family voluntarily submitted to them, but the relationship was strained due to the ties Angel established between his family and the Blinders' rivals. To make matters worse, Angel fell in love ofLizzie Stark, the secretary ofThomas Shelby, the gang's leader. Angel and Lizzie began dating.

characters.name: Anna Gray
characters.description: Anna GrayBiographical informationBornMay 15, 1906AgeUnknown[1]OriginBirmingham, EnglandPersonal informationStatusDeceasedDiedBefore 1918Cause/reasonSpring feverRelationshipsParentsMr. Gray †(Father)Polly Gray†(Mother)Unnamed adoptive parentsBrothersMichael Gray†UnclesArthur Shelby Sr.†NephewsLaurence GrayFirst cousinsArthur Shelby†Thomas Shelby†John Shelby†Ada Thorne†Finn ShelbyOther relativesKatie ShelbyKarl ThorneCharles ShelbyBilly ShelbyRuby Shelby†Military serviceAllegianceShelby FamilyAppearance informationFirst mentionedEpisode 1.6Last mentionedEpisode 4.2Mentioned in5 episodesClassificationMentioned Polly Gray†(Mother)Unnamed adoptive parents Anna Graywas the daughter ofPolly Shelbyand Mr. Gray. She was taken away from her mother by the parish authorities. She's never met her mother again and died in Australia. According to an anecdote by Polly in 1922, Anna would have been eighteen that year making her birth year 1906 on May 15. Having been taken away from Polly alongside her brotherMichael Gray, the siblings were separated. She was originally put with a family in Stafford with a station master as adopted father. However, she kept trying to run away, even trying to reachBirminghamby train. She was eventually sent to Australia where she supposedly died of "Spring Fever," a mosquito-borne illness with symptoms akin to influenza and arthritis. Her body's buried there.Thomastells Polly that Anna was never able to settle and tried multiple times to return to England. In 1922 Polly, her mother, kept having a dream about Anna as a girl with dark eyes told her that she's passed. over. Polly believes that when she was inside the noose, she saw spirits and her second sight came back. She says she saw Anna's spirit and hope living with her son and her dead daughter in Australia.

characters.name: Anton Kaledin
characters.description: Anton KaledinSeries3First seenEpisode 3.1Last seenEpisode 3.1Appeared in1 episodeAge52 (born c. 1872)StatusDeceasedDeath16th February 1924, shot byArthur Shelby Jr.Death shown in episodeEpisode 3.1AllegianceSoviet UnionPortrayed byRichard Brake Anton Kaledinwas a Russian refugee who attempted to discuss business withSabiniandThomas Shelbyon Thomas's wedding day. For his attempts to negotiate business, he was killed byArthur ShelbyJr. He is the opening antagonist of Season 3. He informs Thomas that he and thePeaky Blindersgang have many enemies who are constantly watching them. They discussTatiana Petrovna's meeting with Thomas giving him his payment for which he will carry out work forWinston Churchill.

characters.name: Arthur Shelby
characters.description: Arthur William ShelbyBiographical informationFull nameArthur Shelby Jr.Born14th March 1887Age51OriginSmall Heath, Birmingham, EnglandAlso known asMr. ShelbyArthur Shelby Jr.Personal informationStatusDeceasedDied2nd December 1938Cause/reasonStrangled byThomas ShelbyAffiliationShelby FamilyPeaky BlindersOccupationSoldier(1914-1918)BookmakerCareer CriminalGangsterBusinessman(1926-??)RelationshipsWifeLinda Shelby(1923-1938)FatherArthur Shelby Sr.†MotherMrs. Shelby†BrothersThomas Shelby†John Shelby†Finn ShelbySistersAda Thorne†SonsBilly ShelbyUnclesCharlie Strong(non-biological)AuntsPolly Gray†NephewsDuke ShelbyKarl ThorneCharles ShelbyNiecesKatie ShelbyRuby Shelby†Elizabeth YoungerGrandmothersBirdie Boswell†First cousinsMichael Gray†Anna Gray†Other relativesLaurence GrayBrothers-in-lawFreddie Thorne(1919-1921) †Sisters-in-lawMartha Shelby(??) †Esme Shelby(1919-1924)Grace Shelby(1924) †Lizzie Shelby(1926-)Mary Shelby(1934-)Others in-lawGina Gray(1929-1934)Military serviceAllegianceUnited KingdomService/branchBritish ArmyYears of service1914-1918Unit1/1st Royal Warwickshire RegimentWarsWorld War IBattlesGallipoliThe SommeVerdunAppearance informationSeries1,2,3,4,5,6First seenEpisode 1.1Last seenLock and KeyAppeared in36 episodesPortrayed byPaul AndersonClassificationPresent; main character Arthur Shelby Jr.is one of the main protagonists of thePeaky Blinders. He is the eldest son ofArthurandMrs Shelby, older brother ofThomas,John,AdaandFinn Shelby. Arthur served alongside his brothers Thomas and John during World War I; his experiences during it caused him to suffer constant fits of rage upon returning home, making him a dangerous member of thePeaky Blinders.

characters.name: Barney Thomason
characters.description: Barney ThomasonBiographical informationBornc. 1895Died1929OriginSmall Heath, Birmingham, EnglandAllegiancePeaky BlindersStatusDeceasedMilitary serviceServiceBritish ArmyYears of service1914-1918Unit1/1st Royal Warwickshire RegimentBattlesBattle of the Somme, the Great WarAppearance informationSeries5First seenThe ShockLast seenMr JonesAppeared in2 episodesPortrayed byCosmo JarvisBarney Thomason was a WWI comrade and old friend ofThomas Shelby's. He was admitted to a mental asylum shortly after the war after committing an unknown crime, presumably due to his severe shell-shock and was certified insane. He served as a sniper in WWI, trained by the Royal Marines. He gained a reputation of being a very skilled marksman, and according to Thomas, never missed a target. Prone to manic episodes and hearing voices, Barney was only able to be calmed down by Thomas, for whom Barney had deep respect as Thomas was his superior in the War.

characters.name: Bethany Boswell
characters.description: Bethany BoswellSeries3First seenEpisode 3.3Last seenEpisode 3.3Appeared in1 episodeStatusAliveAllegianceShelby FamilyPortrayed byFrances Tomelty Bethany Boswellis a wise old Gypsy woman living in Wales whoThomas Shelbyseeks out to find out if the sapphire he gave toGracewas indeed cursed as he was told byTatiana Petrovna. She andPolly Shelby's mother,Birdie Boswellhave the same surname. Thomas has hopped in his caravan with his sonCharlesandJohnny Dogs, and is heading to Wales with the ‘cursed’ sapphire necklace. There he meets with Bethany Boswell, a gypsy who he asks to confirm that the piece of jewellery is bestowed with some sinister force, not necessarily because he believes it, but to be absolved of his guilt. Leaving the necklace with the woman, he’s able to return to some sense of normality.

ScanlanClassificationPresent; recurring character Billy Gradeis an Irish retired professional footballer who becomes a singer inthe Garrison Pub. He becomes a bookmaker for thePeaky Blinders. Billy had to retire from football due to a leg injury. He maintains his contacts with the sport.

characters.name: Billy Kimber
characters.description: Billy KimberBiographical informationBorn7 February 1882, Aston, BirminghamDied3 December 1919,Small Heath, Birmingham(aged 37)OriginBirmingham, EnglandAllegianceBirmingham BoysStatusDeceasedAppearance informationSeries1First seenEpisode 1.2Last seenEpisode 1.6Appeared in4 episodesPortrayed byCharlie Creed-MilesWilliam "Billy" Kimberwas an English gangster and head of the Birmingham Boys, the main enemy ofThomas Shelbyand thePeaky Blinders. He ran most of the legal tracks outside ofLondon, leading to tensions between Kimber and the Peaky Blinders, who looked to expand their control into inner city London. Due to his wealth and influence, Kimber had policemen on his payroll and an assistant,Roberts. He serves as the secondary antagonist of thefirst series. Kimber clawed his way from a life of poverty in the slums to become the premier gangster in Birmingham. He is now rich and semi-legitimate. He controls the legal and illegal bookmaking sites at racecourses across England. After finding out that his races were fixed by the Peaky Blinders, Billy Kimber decides to pay them a visit to tell them he will shoot the leader, Thomas Shelby, in the head inEpisode 1.2. Thomas treats him with respect and offers him an opportunity for them to work together, and Billy eventually agrees. He gets his assistant Roberts to handle the rest of the meeting as he leaves, and Roberts states that they will be at Cheltenham to further discuss business.

characters.name: Billy Kitchen
characters.description: Billy KitchenSeries2First seenEpisode 2.3Last seenEpisode 2.5Appeared in3 episodesTitlesHead BakerAgeApprox. 35 (born c. 1886)StatusDeceasedDeathJanuary 1922, shot byAlfie SolomonsDeath shown in episodeEpisode 2.5OriginBirmingham, EnglandAllegiancePeaky BlindersSiblingsUnnamed brothers and cousinsBetty Kitchen (relative, episode 3.3)Portrayed byPaul Bullion Betty Kitchen (relative, episode 3.3) Corporal William "Billy"Kitchenwas a Black Country man who briefly worked as "Head Baker" forThomas ShelbyandAlfie Solomons. While he posed as a baker, he in fact worked undercover as a member of thePeaky Blindersduring their deal with Solomons.

characters.name: Billy Shelby
characters.description: Billy ShelbyBiographical informationFull nameWilliam Arthur ShelbyBornSeptember 1924Age29OriginBirmingham, EnglandAlso known asBillyPersonal informationStatusAliveAffiliationShelby Family(formely)RelationshipsFatherArthur Shelby†MotherLinda ShelbyUnclesThomas Shelby†John Shelby†Finn ShelbyAuntsAda Thorne†Great-unclesCharlie Strong(non-biological)Great-auntsPolly Gray†GrandfathersArthur Shelby Sr.†GrandmothersMrs. Shelby†Great-grandfathersMr. Shelby †Great-grandmothersBirdie Boswell†First cousinsKatie ShelbyKarl ThorneCharles ShelbyRuby Shelby†Other relativesMichael Gray†Anna Gray†Laurence GrayMilitary serviceAllegianceShelby Family(formely)Appearance informationSeries4First seenThe NooseLast seenBlackbirdAppeared in5 episodesPortrayed byEtahan Picard-Edwards(Series 4)Andrew Jefferson-Tierney(Series 4)ClassificationFormer; recurring characterWilliam Arthur ‘Billy’ Shelbyis the son of the decasedArthurand his wifeLinda Shelby. Andrew Jefferson-Tierney(Series 4) On the first episode of season 4, Billy is seen being held by his fatherArthur Shelby, outside in their garden collecting chicken eggs.Linda Shelbythen comes outside reminding Arthur to be careful with Billy around the chickens, and to not smoke near him. Later on in the season, Billy is seen being held by his mother, in the living room of their house in Birmingham. Arthur then comes home drunk, so Linda puts Billy to bed upstairs. Billy is last seen being held by his mother, at his supposedly dead father’s funeral.

She married a Shelby, the father ofArthurandPolly Shelby. Polly tells her sonMichael Graythat his grandmother was a Gypsy Princess. Polly tellsRuben Oliverthat her mother stole the  expensive dress from a house she was cleaning in 1901. She andBethany Boswell, who is the wise Gypsy, have the same surname. Pollytells her mother's name. Polly tells her sonMichael Graythat his grandmother was a Gypsy Princess. characters.name: Bonnie Gold
characters.description: Bonnie GoldBiographical informationBorn1900Age29OriginEngland, United KingdomPersonal informationStatusDeceasedDied4 November 1929Cause/reasonShot in the head byJimmy McCavernAffiliationPeaky BlindersOccupationBoxerRelationshipsFatherAberama Gold†MotherMrs. Gold†SistersEsmeralda GoldUnnamed sisterUnnamed sisterAppearance informationFirst seenHeathensLast seenBlack CatsAppeared in5 episodesPortrayed byJack RowanClassificationFormer; recurring character Bonnie Goldwas a young Gypsy man and the son ofAberama Gold. He was a fierce fighter and had ambitions of joining the exclusive world of professional boxing. He becomes a Peaky Blinder in 1926 but is killed byJimmy McCavernin 1929. Bonnie Gold is a Gypsy boy from a dangerous family, the son of an assassin, a powerful manAberama Gold. He has no intention of following in the family footsteps. His objective is to become a boxing champion. His father Aberama says Bonnie's got his dad's strength and his mother's temper.

characters.name: Charlie Chaplin
characters.description: Charlie ChaplinBiographical informationFull nameCharles Spencer ChaplinDate of birth16 April 1889Place of birthBlack Patch, Smethwick, EnglandAge44StatusAliveOccupationActorComedianDirectorComposerRelativesParentsCharles Chaplin Sr.†Hannah Hill†WifeMildred Harris(1918 - 1920)Lita Gray(1924 - 1927)ChildrenNorman Chaplin†Charles Chaplin Jr.Sydney ChaplinAppearance informationSeries2First appearanceEpisode 2.5Last mentionedBlack DayAppeared in1 episodeMentioned in1 episodePortrayed byRobert Elkin Charles Spencer“Charlie”Chaplinis an English comic actor, filmmaker and composer, who rose to fame in the era ofsilent film. He appeared inEpisode 2.5and was portrayed by Robert Elkin. Charles was born on 16 April 1889 and according toThomas Shelby, he was born atBlack Patch, a Gypsy camp inBirmingham. Charles is a Romany Gypsy, but hides it from the public. Thomas knew Charles through his bodyguard, Wag MacDonald, who is also Romany Gypsy.
